mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate above 5.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening demand for goods and services that reduces corporate profits and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts above 1.1 million signals an increase in supply of new homes that reduces housing prices and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts above 1.1 million are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month rise in the CPI above 2.5% signal a monetary easing cycle and inflationary pressures that increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining federal funds rate and rising CPI above 2.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in real personal income above 4% signals an increase in consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real personal income above 4% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in initial unemployment claims below 250,000 signals a strengthening labor market that reduces layoffs and increases consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining initial unemployment claims below 250,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in industrial production and a 2-month decline in housing starts signal an increase in supply of goods and services and a decrease in supply of new homes, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising industrial production and declining housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
